zh.lifeSummary: 當Pedro Fernandes de Bragança於1113年出生在葡萄牙比拉 雷亞爾安踏時，他的父親Fernão Mendes de Bragança19歲，母親Theresa Soares de Maia33歲。他和Fruilhe Sanches de Barbosa至少有7個兒子和5個女兒。他去世於1195年享年82歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Pedro Fernandes de Bragança于1113年出生在葡萄牙比拉 雷亞爾安踏时，他的父亲Fernão Mendes de Bragança19岁，母亲Theresa Soares de Maia33岁。他和Fruilhe Sanches de Barbosa至少有7个儿子和5个女儿。他去世于1195年享年82岁。

nameMeanings.name: Fernandes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Carlos, Luis, Fernando, Jorge, Mario, Francisco, Orlando, Ana, Abilio, Americo.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Henrique, Ilidio, Paulo, Agostinho, Amadeu, Manoel, Aderito, Lourenco, Marcio, Serafim.

Portuguese and Galician: patronymic from the personal name   Fernando  . This is one of the most common surnames in Portugal. It is also found in Goa and elsewhere on the west coast of India, having been taken there by Portuguese colonists.
Spanish: variant or altered form of Fernández (see Fernandez ).

variants.name: Fernando
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Luis, Orlando, Aida, Carlos, Jesusa, Rodolfo, Ruben, Adela, Alfredo, Alicia.  Indian Rohan, Lalith, Nimal, Rajiv, Sanjeeva, Shanthi, Anil, Anusha, Dushan, Kapila, Lakshman, Mallika.

Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Fernando, a younger form of Ferdinando (see Ferdinand ). This surname is also found in southern Italy, mainly in Naples and Palermo, since the period of Spanish dominance there, and as a result of the expulsion of the Jews from Spain and Portugal at the end of the 15th century, many of whom moved to Italy. It is also common in western India and especially in Sri Lanka, where it was taken by Portuguese colonists in the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bragança
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Portuguese Bragança: habitational name from the city of Bragança in northern Portugal. This form of the surname is most common in India, as a result of former Portuguese colonization of the western coast of this country, and in the Philippines.

History: Braganza is an Anglicized form of the name of the royal house Bragança whose members occupied the throne of Portugal from 1640 to 1910. Before that, they exercised enormous power in the Iberian peninsula as Dukes of Braganza from 1442 onward.

nameMeanings.name: Pedro
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Pedro Fernandes de Bragança was born in 1113, in Anta, Vila Real, Portugal, his father, Fernão Mendes de Bragança, was 19 and his mother, Theresa Soares de Maia, was 33. He had at least 7 sons and 5 daughters with Fruilhe Sanches de Barbosa. He died in 1195, at the age of 82.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Pedro Fernandes de Bragança est né en 1113, à Anta, Vila Real, Portugal, son père, Fernão Mendes de Bragança, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Theresa Soares de Maia, avait 33 ans. Il a eu au moins 7 fils et 5 filles avec Fruilhe Sanches de Barbosa. Il est décédé en 1195, à l’âge de 82 ans.

Lui avuto almeno 7 figli e 5 figlie con Fruilhe Sanches de Barbosa. Morì nel 1195, all'età di 82 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Pedro Fernandes de Bragança(이)가 1113년 포르투갈 빌라 리 앤타에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Fernão Mendes de Bragança(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Theresa Soares de Maia(은)는 33세였다. 그는 Fruilhe Sanches de Barbosa(와)과 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1195년 82세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Pedro Fernandes de Bragança родился в 1113 г. в местности анта, вила риль, Португалия, его отцу по имени Fernão Mendes de Bragança было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Theresa Soares de Maia, 33. У него было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 5 дочерей от Fruilhe Sanches de Barbosa. Он умер в 1195 г. в возрасте 82 лет.
